questions.question: What are the two most plentiful gases in the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up 78% and Oxygen makes up 21% of the air we breathe.

questions.question: Why is Carbon dioxide called a greenhouse gas?
questions.correct_answer: Because it traps heat radiated from the earth
questions.explanation: By creating a greenhouse effect, it keeps the earth warm enough to sustain life; without it, the earth would be too cold.

questions.question: Name the five layers of the atmosphere starting from the earth's surface.
questions.correct_answer: Troposphere, Stratosphere, Mesosphere, Thermosphere, and Exosphere
questions.explanation: These layers are divided based on temperature conditions and altitude.

questions.question: In which layer of the atmosphere is the Ozone layer found?
questions.explanation: The ozone layer protects us from the harmful effects of the sun's ultraviolet rays.

questions.question: What is the importance of the Troposphere?
questions.correct_answer: It is the layer where we breathe and where all weather phenomena occur
questions.explanation: Almost all weather changes like rainfall, fog, and hailstorms happen in this layer.

questions.question: Define 'Insolation'.
questions.correct_answer: The incoming solar energy intercepted by the earth
questions.explanation: The amount of insolation decreases from the equator towards the poles.

questions.question: What is Air Pressure?
questions.correct_answer: The pressure exerted by the weight of air on the earth's surface
questions.explanation: As we go up the layers of the atmosphere, the pressure falls rapidly.

questions.question: What are the three types of winds?
questions.correct_answer: Permanent winds, Seasonal winds, and Local winds
questions.explanation: Permanent winds blow constantly; Seasonal winds change direction; Local winds blow only during specific periods.

questions.question: What is 'Loo'?
questions.correct_answer: A hot and dry local wind of the northern plains of India
questions.explanation: It is a characteristic local wind that blows during the summer season.

questions.question: What are the three types of rainfall?
questions.explanation: These are classified based on the mechanism of how the air rises and cools to form rain.

notes.title: Global Warming
notes.content: When the level of $CO_2$ increases due to factory smoke or car fumes, the heat retained increases the temperature of the earth.

notes.title: Ionosphere
notes.content: A part of the Thermosphere that helps in radio transmission by reflecting radio waves back to earth.

notes.title: Wind Direction
notes.content: Wind is named after the direction from which it blows, e.g., a 'Westerly' blows from the West.

facts.fact: On the moon, there is no air and hence no air pressure.
Meteorites burn up in the Mesosphere on entering from space.
Plants need nitrogen for their survival, but they cannot take it directly from the air.
